Football.title: Why a Man Utd signing hasn’t played all season and is training with U21s
Football.content:
LEFT-BACK is in danger of becoming a problem position again for Manchester United.

Luke Shaw, the only United player who has started in all 30 of their Premier League games, has been forced off in the last two matches.





Patrick Dorgu is expected to be sidelined until next month with a hamstring injury and his long-term future could be as a winger.

Noussair Mazraoui, a right back by trade, also made way in the 2-1 defeat at Newcastle. His replacement, Tyrell Malacia, emerged for only his second appearance of the season.

Mazraoui’s enforced removal in the second half cost United. Malacia allowed William Osula too much space down United’s right-hand side and then failed to close him down.

Osula whipped the ball into the far corner for a glorious 90th-minute winner.



United will release Malacia when his contract expires in the summer after four unhappy years.

The club blocked Malacia’s intended move to a Turkish club on transfer deadline day in February and he was the only member of the five-man ‘bomb squad’ who failed to secure a transfer in the summer.

Malacia, 26, missed the entirety of the 2023-24 season with a knee injury that required two rounds of surgery. He went 550 days without playing for United.

Since the Dutchman’s return to the fold in November 2024, he has made ten appearances for the club.



Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets on day one

Malacia was eventually restored to first-team training in late September.

When Malacia was named on the bench against Brighton in late-October, Shaw, Dorgu and Mazraoui were also in the squad.

Diego Leon wasn’t.

It is easy to forget that United signed a left back in the summer, in the 18-year-old Paraguayan

Leon was actually paraded on the Old Trafford pitch alongside Matheus Cunha, Bryan Mbeumo and Benjamin Sesko before United’s final pre-season friendly with Fiorentina.



Affording Leon that privilege was clearly premature when he has not had a look-in in five months.

The Paraguayan was an unused substitute four times under Ruben Amorim but he has not made the bench since the 2-0 defeat of Sunderland on October 4.



That was Malacia’s first week back in team training.

Amorim then opted for the seniority of Malacia over the raw Leon as cover for Dorgu and Diogo Dalot, United’s left wingers when they operated in a 3-4-2-1 formation.

Leon has not been visible in any training imagery at Carrington since Christmas Eve, either.

There were murmurings he could head out on loan but instead he has been a regular for the under 21s, starting in seven of their past eight games.

United believe it is more logical that Leon trains with the under-21s as that is where his playing opportunities lie. He is still using first team facilities.



Amorim said during pre-season that Leon “is going to be a very good player”.

He added: “You can see it. He’s a smart guy. He’s a guy that came from Paraguay to come to the Premier League. He’s really powerful.”

It was a positive pre-season for United and Amorim probably got carried away. Leon cameoed twice against West Ham in New Jersey and Bournemouth in Chicago, the latter his last first-team minutes.

Leon started in United’s first friendly against Leeds United in Stockholm but struggled and he has not convinced at reserve level.

He showed a lack of positional awareness on his debut in Sweden, though nerves are forgivable for a rookie playing for sport’s most scrutinised team.



Playing U21 football can be a thankless task but Shea Lacey’s form earned him a promotion. Leon has never looked like returning to the fold.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Malacia became such a fixture in Amorim’s squads he was on the bench in the Portuguese’s last nine games in charge.

He has made the bench in every one of Michael Carrick’s games in the dugout.

Football.title: Tottenham played secret role in Arsenal transfer for Piero Hincapie before Gunners swooped to sign defender
Football.content:
ARSENAL hijacked Tottenham’s move for Eberechi Eze last summer – and they reportedly rushed into landing Piero Hincapie after Spurs set their sights on a deal.

The defender, 24, joined the Gunners on an initial loan from Bayer Leverkusen.





And Arsenal will trigger their £45million option to turn Hincapie’s switch into a permanent agreement at the end of the season.

But the Premier League table-toppers only swooped for the Ecuadorian when it became clear that he was on Tottenham’s list of targets.

Fabrizio Romano reports that Spurs were prepared to sanction a move which would have seen Hincapie join immediately.

But that led to Arsenal rushing into offering Leverkusen a structured agreement which would still guarantee a permanent exit from Germany.



It means that Tottenham played a secret role in the Gunners getting their hands on Hincapie, who has gone on to become boss Mikel Arteta’s first-choice left-back.

The Bundesliga title winner has made 27 appearances since his arrival at the Emirates Stadium.

And Hincapie’s form has been key in Arsenal challenging for a historic quadruple.

Fellow suitors Tottenham are desperately trying to claw themselves away from a humiliating relegation to the Championship after missing out.



Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets on day one

Thomas Frank, who was in charge of Spurs when they came calling for Hincapie’s signature, was brutally axed in February.

Igor Tudor was brought in as the Dane’s replacement until the end of the season – but he has suffered defeat in every showdown so far.

It has left Tottenham just ONE POINT adrift of the Premier League’s dreaded drop zone.



Spurs missed out on Hincapie after also being beaten by their arch-rivals in the race for Eze.

The England man, 27, snubbed Tottenham when Arsenal came calling and sealed a £67.5m move from Crystal Palace.

Football.title: Watch moment Mikel Arteta chases Declan Rice through tunnel of slaps as Arsenal prepare for Champions League clash
Football.content:
ARSENAL kicked off their Champions League last-16 preparations with a friendly round of slaps on Tuesday.

The Gunners face Bayer Leverkusen away from home in the first round of knockout games on Wednesday.





They had their final training session before flying to Germany on Tuesday.

And before things got started, Mikel Arteta jokingly chased Declan Rice through a tunnel of slaps.

Arsenal stars lined up on either side of the tunnel before hitting the midfielder on the head as he ran through.



Arteta then pushed Eberechi Eze through the tunnel.

Noni Madueke – who was celebrating his 24th birthday – and Christian Norgaard also got slapped by their team-mates ahead of the Champions League meeting.

The Gunners go into the clash with a 100 per cent win record in Europe this season.

They managed eight wins from eight in the league phase to top the table, scoring 23 goals and conceding just four.



William Saliba, Leandro Trossard and Riccardo Calafiori are expected to be available for Arsenal against Bayer Leverkusen despite injury doubts.

Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets on day one

All three trained in front of the media with the rest of the squad on Tuesday.

Meanwhile, Martin Odegaard and Ben White were both absent and are expected to miss out, with Mikel Merino still recovering from a long-term foot injury.

David Raya, Gabriel Magalhaes, Declan Rice and Martin Zubimendi were all rested for Saturday’s trip to Mansfield Town in the FA Cup – which resulted in a 2-1 win – and will likely start.

Football.title: Cristiano Ronaldo gets bizarre ‘pressotherapy’ treatment to save World Cup dream with injury ‘worse than first feared’
Football.content:
CRISTIANO RONALDO has turned to ‘pressotherapy’ treatment in a bid to save his World Cup dream.

The Manchester United and Real Madrid hero sparked fears he could be denied a record SIXTH tournament after hobbling off in the Saudi Pro League.





The Portugal superstar, 41, picked up a muscle injury during Al-Nassr’s recent 3-1 win over Al-Feyha.

After limping off, he was spotted with an ice pack on the back of his knee as he took his spot on the bench.

He has since left Saudi Arabia to return to Spain for treatment with his injury worse than first feared.

His road to recovery is underway as he shared a picture of himself undergoing the treatment.

It is a procedure that is said to help with lymphatic drainage and it also eases aches and pains and detoxifying the body.

We told you last week that his club boss Jorge Jesus confirmed the injury was worse than initially feared.

He stated: “After tests, we see the injury suffered by Cristiano Ronaldo was more serious than expected.

“Cristiano will now travel to Spain, like other players who went for treatment when they were injured.



Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets on day one

“His injury required treatment in Madrid with his personal therapist, and we hope he returns quickly and helps the team”.

Portugal face Mexico and the United States in friendlies over the upcoming international break at the end of the month and it remains to be seen if Ronaldo will be fit to be considered.

He will be hoping his treatment gets him back on the field as soon as possible to help Al-Nassr in their SPL title bid – and add to his impressive goal tally.

He’s netted a stunning 21 goals in 22 league outings to date with his side top of the standings by two points.

Football.title: PSG unveil stunning new ‘night edition’ jersey for Chelsea clash as they seek revenge after Club World Cup hammering
Football.content:
PSG have released a FIFTH kit of the season right before they host Chelsea in the Champions League.

The French giants are aiming to get one back over their rivals of the past 12 years in the last-16 first leg on Wednesday night, and will do so in style.





Chelsea travel to Paris to face the current European champions and do so with the bragging rights after July’s Club World Cup demolition in New York.

But PSG, who have worked with Jordan – part of Nike – previously, are out for revenge and will rock a fresh top for the occasion.

On Monday, the Parisians unveiled yet another flashy match shirt through their Jordan crossover.

The “Night Edition” jersey is a slick black strip with bolts of light and electricity inspired by activity after dark.

It is described as “capturing the energy of Paris at night” with stars Ousmane Dembele, Marquinhos, and Desire Doue all styling the out-there number.

The kit includes iridescent Jordan and club logos as well as streaky patches of gold.

Like many of their other Jordan efforts, PSG’s latest flary design has gone down well online.



One user wrote on X: “PSG and Jordan collab always brings the fire.”

Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets on day one

Another added: “Wow what a beautiful jersey.”

A third said simply: “Aura.”

It is part of an entire range revealed this week by the club as they head into two huge UCL matches.

PSG already have a ‘normal’ white away shirt and red away top.



Their black fourth jersey is also from Jordan but has been made redundant by the new lights design.

Chelsea will be wearing their white away shirt with an ode to the 1970s.

Despite the two teams playing in blue/navy, neither will be repping their usual colours at the Parc des Princes.

The game is a replay of the CWC final which Chelsea won 3-0.

Enzo Maresca was in charge at the time and set his side up to dominate the recently crowned UCL champs.



Cole Palmer and Joao Pedro lethally cut through PSG on a baking afternoon in America.

Tensions boiled over at full-time with PSG boss Luis Enrique slapping Joao Pedro and the players involved in a mini scrap.

Chelsea stars announced themselves as being ready for another fight with the team they faced three times in as many seasons between 2014 and 2016.

A modern rivalry was built and continued in USA.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Neither side has been in top form since with PSG involved in a tighter than expected battle to win Ligue 1 and Chelsea moving on from Maresca at the turn of the year.



Familiar face Liam Rosenior will be the man in the dugout over the next week.

Football.title: PSG president Nasser Al-Khelaifi stranded in Qatar due to Iranian war and in desperate scramble to make Chelsea clash
Football.content:
PARIS SAINT-GERMAIN president Nasser Al-Khelaifi could miss their Champions League clash with Chelsea due to the Iranian war.

The Qatari businessman, 52, is stuck in Doha having returned to his home country last week to be with his family.





Doha has been targeted by the Iranian regime with missiles and drones in retaliation to American and Israeli attacks.

Al-Khelaifi was absent from PSG’s Paris training base on Tuesday and may not be able to make it back to the French capital in time for Wednesday’s last-16 first leg at Parc des Princes.

The PSG president is rarely absent from matches and is working to find a solution to be in the stands against Liam Rosenior’s side on Wednesday.

Qatar airspace has been closed since the conflict began on February 28, though limited security corridors have now been put in place to allow a small number of flights.



Qatar Airways have only been operating limited repatriation flights out of Doha’s Hamad International Airport.

The Qatari capital is still currently set to host the Finalissima between Argentina and Spain later this month – with a number of other games also planned as part of the Qatar Football Festival.

Egypt and Serbia, as well as Qatar, are all due to play games during the March international break.

Uefa said last week that a new venue for the Finalissima was not currently being considered though a decision is expected by the end of this week.



Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets on day one

However, reports have suggested that Wembley or Benfica’s Estadio da Luz are being considered.

A Uefa statement read: “Discussions are ongoing with the local organisers, who have made a tremendous effort to ensure the match is a success.

“A final decision is expected by the end of next week.

“At this stage, ‌no ⁠alternative venue is being considered.”

Football.title: Arsenal wonderkid Ethan Nwaneri’s loan transfer turns into ‘nightmare’ but Arteta can’t recall him despite injury crisis
Football.content:
ARSENAL thought they had secured the perfect move for Ethan Nwaneri when they sent him on loan to Marseille in January.

The 18-year-old was struggling for game-time in North London and a switch to France promised regular minutes under a manager with a reputation for exciting football and impressive youth development.



Now, just six weeks on, the French press has described Nwaneri’s loan as a “nightmare” following a snowball of unfortunate events.



The teenager’s move hit its first stumbling block when Marseille boss Roberto De Zerbi was sacked, just 18 days after Nwaneri’s arrival.

Nwaneri had started every game under De Zerbi and even scored a stunning long-range effort on his Ligue 1 debut.

But De Zerbi was cut loose in a messy axing due to a string of poor results, leaving Nwaneri in limbo until Marseille found a new boss.

De Zerbi’s presence at the club and his style of football were key to Arsenal’s decision to send Nwaneri on loan to Marseille – a decision made after extensive talks between the club, the player’s agent and his father.



Mikel Arteta had described De Zerbi as an “incredible developer of young talent” when Nwaneri’s loan was first announced, and Arsenal considered the former Brighton boss a huge draw for the switch.

To make matters worse, De Zerbi’s dismissal coincided with a midfield injury crisis in North London.

Mikel Merino suffered a fracture in his foot, Martin Odegaard was sidelined with two issues and Kai Havertz had a setback in his return to action.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

Those injury blows would have likely seen Nwaneri jump up the pecking order if he were still in N5, but Arteta insisted he has no regrets over the transfer.

Speaking after Arsenal’s 1-1 draw with Brentford last month, the Arsenal manager said: “After making the decision, who could have predicted that Mikel was going to be out for five months and Kai?

“It’s unfortunate, in the end we have to make decisions in the moment, understanding the context there.

“The best decision for the club, for Ethan, was for him to go and experience a different environment, have minutes played, and get ready to come back to us.”



But you have to wonder whether that stance may now have changed.

Nwaneri hasn’t started a game for Marseille since De Zerbi’s dismissal and has twice missed out on minutes altogether under new boss Habib Beye.

His latest outing ended in disaster, too.

Nwaneri missed the decisive penalty that sent Marseille crashing out of the Coupe de France in the quarter-finals against Toulouse.

The youngster blasted his spot-kick over the bar as Marseille lost the shoot-out 4-3 following a 2-2 draw in regulation time.



The misplaced penalty has led French outlet Foot Mercato to claim that Nwaneri’s “adventure” on the continent has turned into a “disaster.”

Of course, there have been frustrating moments – including a rash tackle against Rennes that he was lucky not to be sent off for – but most of what has happened to Nwaneri appears to be bad luck.

He has contributed on the pitch, too. Providing the game-winning assist for Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang against Lyon last week.



And he cannot be blamed for De Zerbi’s exit, the lottery of a shootout or the fact Arsenal have had an injury crisis back home.

The unfortunate thing for Nwaneri, and the Gunners, is that his contract does not contain a break clause.

This means Arsenal have no way of recalling him from his loan until the summer, even if they desperately wanted to end it early.

SunSport previously revealed that SIX Premier League teams were interested in taking Nwaneri on loan in January, and perhaps that would have proved more beneficial in hindsight.

The other stroke of bad luck is right on Nwaneri’s doorstep.



Max Dowman, 16, is catching the eye at Arsenal and was the talk of the town after his impressive performance against Mansfield at the weekend.

A left-footed attacking midfielder who can operate off the right flank like Nwaneri, Dowman is yet another barrier to entry into the Arsenal first-team for Nwaneri.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Summer arrivals of Noni Madueke from Chelsea and Ebere Eze from Crystal Palace also blocked potential avenues into the team for the England youth international.

Nwaneri made 37 appearances for the Gunners last term, scoring nine times, but it now feels hard to see that happening again any time soon.

Football.title: Real Madrid without SEVEN first-team stars for Man City clash with Jude Bellingham and Kylian Mbappe leading absentees
Football.content:
REAL MADRID will be without SEVEN first team players against Manchester City tomorrow, including Jude Bellingham and Kylian Mbappe.

The Spanish giants go up against Pep Guardiola’s men in the knock-out stages for the sixth time in the last seven seasons.





But Alvaro Arbeloa’s options are limited with several of his key players sidelined by injury for the first leg at the Bernabeu.

Mbappe is back in Madrid after visiting a specialist in Paris about the knee injury which has kept him out of the last three games.

He was absent from training on Tuesday morning – along with England’s Jude Bellingham who is nursing a hamstring injury.

There is some hope Mbappe could be back for the return at the Etihad next Tuesday – but Bellingham is rated doubtful for both with a return against Atletico Madrid on March 22 more likely.



Rodrygo – who has shone against City in the past – underwent surgery on a cruciate knee ligament injury earlier this week and is out for the rest of 2026.

Former Preston full-back Alvaro Carreras is the latest casualty and misses out along with fellow defenders David Alaba, Eder Militao and midfielder Dani Ceballos.

None of the seven trained with their team-mates at their Valdebebas HQ on Tuesday morning, though Mbappe trained alone, and the coach is planning without them for the first leg against City.

Coach Arbeloa said: “It’s clear we have some absences who are top level players – some of the best – but we are Real Madrid and not underdogs.



Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets on day one

“It doesn’t matter about the situation – we don’t need to feel inferior to any team we are facing. We will go for the win.

“Mbappe is recovering quite well, we have to see day by day about his recovery. He is back in great spirits and we hope he can be back as soon as possible.”

Skipper Fede Valverde gave Madrid a much needed lift by scoring a late winner against Celta Vigo at the weekend.



But he admits his weakened team will have to be ‘perfect’ if they are to come out on top against City over the two legs.

He said: “Mbappe is really important but now we are preparing and have to focus – we are working with the players who will be on the pitch.

“We give our best in defence as well and have to get a result to take to the second leg. We’ll have to be physically and mentally perfect to do it.”

Football.title: Joey Barton remanded in custody over 'golf club attack'
Football.content:
Former footballer Joey Barton has been remanded in custody after appearing at Liverpool magistrates charged with assault following a fight at a golf club.

Barton wearing glasses, a blue top and white trousers, spoke only to confirm his name and address. Relatives of the two accused sat at the back of the court. The 43-year-old, from Widnes, Cheshire, is accused of assault occasioning grievous bodily harm along with a second man, Gary O’Grady, 50, from Huyton, Merseyside.

The case was sent to Liverpool Crown Court, where the men will appear for a plea hearing on April 7.

District Judge James Hatton told Barton, who wore a navy blue jumper, he would be remanded in custody until that hearing. O’Grady, in a grey tracksuit, was released on conditional bail.

Image: Liverpool Echo) Liverpool Echo)

The men are accused of attacking Kevin Lynch at Fairway, Huyton, on Sunday. Barton had been arrested following the assault at Huyton and Prescot Golf Club on Sunday evening. Merseyside Police officers found a man with injuries to his face and ribs, who was later taken to hospital for further treatment.

The victim remains in hospital in a "serious but stable condition," police added. Earlier, at a High Court hearing on Tuesday over a libel claim involving Barton, Gervase de Wilde, representing former England footballer Eni Aluko, said his instructing solicitor had “received a call from a duty solicitor in a police station in Liverpool” shortly before the hearing.

Mr de Wilde told the court on Tuesday that Barton "was arrested yesterday and is currently being held in custody." Merseyside Police said in a statement: "We can confirm that two men have been charged following an assault in Huyton on Sunday night, 8 March.

Image: PA) PA)

At around 9pm, emergency services were called to Fairway to reports of a man having been assaulted near to Huyton and Prescot Golf Club.

The victim was taken to hospital for assessment of serious injuries to his face and body. He is in a serious but stable condition in hospital. Two men were arrested. Detectives have now charged the following men with Section 18 wounding with intent: Joseph Anthony Barton, 43, of Fox Bank Close, Widnes. Gary O’Grady, 50, of Seel Road, Huyton.

Image: Liverpool Echo) Liverpool Echo)

"Both men remain in custody to appear at Wirral Magistrates Court this afternoon, Tuesday 10 March." Police erected a cordon on Fairway in the Knowsley town on Sunday night. Barton is a previous football player known over his appearances for Newcastle United, Manchester United and Burnley.

He made one appearance for England before going on to manage Fleetwood Town and Bristol Rovers.

Football.title: Arsenal and Liverpool transfer target responds to Premier League interest
Football.content:
Arsenal and Liverpool transfer target responds to Premier League interest

Frankfurt defender Nnamdi Collins has been linked with a Premier League switch as the likes of Arsenal and Liverpool consider the young German, who has made his stance clear

View 2 Images Nnamdi Collins is being linked with a Frankfurt exit

Frankfurt defender Nnamdi Collins admits he's "surprised" after reading rumours about a possible move to Premier League giants Liverpool or Arsenal.

‌



The 22-year-old has continued to impress in Germany, leading to the Bundesliga outfit slapping a €40million (£34m) price tag on him. Collins has become integral under new coach Albert Riera, who has made him central to his back four.

‌



Frankfurt have continued to sell their top players for the right fee - losing both Omar Marmoush and Hugo Ekitike over the past 12 months. The latter moved to Liverpool and the Reds are linked with another swoop for a player from Deutsche Bank Park.

‌



Collins himself has paid little to no attention to talk of him making a move to England with the 22-year-old, who came through the ranks at Borussia Dortmund, underlining his happiness at Frankfurt.

He told Absolut Fussball: "It actually bounces off me pretty well. I’m reading these rumours for the first time myself, which is why I’m partly surprised. I’m very happy to be here at Eintracht."

Chelsea's stance on selling Liam Delap after scoring once since summer transfer READ MORE :

Pep Lijnders to manage Man City against Liverpool after Pep Guardiola banned for FA Cup tie READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



Liverpool could be on the lookout for defensive reinforcements with Ibrahima Konate still yet to sign a new deal at Anfield. His exit would leave a sizeable void whilst Joe Gomez is another defender with an uncertain future. The Reds rejigged their backline last summer, bringing in two new full-backs.

Arsenal meanwhile have a more settled backline, which is regarded as the best in Europe. The likes of William Saliba, Gabriel and Jurrien Timber are quality operators and any new defender would need to push hard for a regular starting spot.

View 2 Images Konate is out of contract at the end of the season (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Collins, capable of playing centrally and at right-back, would result in a huge profit if he did leave the club. Frankfurt have also managed to land huge profits in the past from moving on the likes of Luka Jovic, Sebastien Haller and Randal Kolo Muani with CEO Axel Hellmann explaining how they keep finding gems.

“We have one of the best coaching teams in the Bundesliga,” he told The Athletic. “That’s really important, because we sign young players and the process of educating them, which is what is making us famous at the moment, involves a lot. We are open with our strategy when we talk to the players and to other clubs.

"That’s the reason why we are well connected to other clubs, because they know we take that work seriously. We are an international club. We have the airport just around the corner. Frankfurt is an international city with 147 different nationalities. It doesn’t matter if you come from South America or Africa, you have your community here.”

Football.title: Man Utd set sights on Andoni Iraola but face competition from three Premier League rivals
Football.content:
Man Utd set sights on Andoni Iraola but face competition from three Premier League rivals

Manchester United will appoint a permanent head coach this summer and Bournemouth's Andoni Iraola is one of the names on the radar of the Red Devils and Premier League rivals

View 2 Images Bournemouth head coach Andoni Iraola has plenty of Premier League admirers (Image: Getty Images)

Manchester United and Tottenham are among the Premier League clubs who could pounce if Andoni Iraola decides to leave Bournemouth at the end of the season.

‌



The Cherries head coach, 43, has been a revelation since succeeding Gary O'Neil on the south coast back in the summer of 2023, guiding Bournemouth to their best ever Premier League finish last season. Unfortunately for them, Iraola's work has not gone unnoticed elsewhere and with his contract up this summer, potential suitors are starting to emerge.

‌



A number of top-flight clubs are set to seek new managers ahead of the 2026-27 campaign including Manchester United and Spurs, both of whom are admirers of Iraola according to The i Paper.

‌



They also claim that Crystal Palace - who will replace the outgoing Oliver Glasner at the end of the season - are another team who are keen on Iraola, who has further enhanced his stock over the past six months by keeping Bournemouth on the rise after losing key players.

Glazer family in £223million boost as Man Utd owners continue to see wealth soar READ MORE :

Luis Enrique disagreement emerges as Man Utd left with tough Michael Carrick decision READ MORE :

While Iraola has been discussed in relation to the United and Tottenham vacancies, he is now said to be a direct target for Crystal Palace, as well as on the radar of clubs in Italy and Spain. Intriguingly, Glasner has been touted as a possible target for both United and Spurs, who have made interim appointments to see them through to the summer.

Bournemouth, however, still hope they will be able to persuade Iraola to extend his spell with them despite opening contract talks with the former Rayo Vallecano coach a year ago.

‌



The same report notes that the club plan to re-open contract talks with Iraola during the upcoming international break and hope that Iraola will take note of how the likes of Thomas Frank fared after moving on following success at a stable club.

View 2 Images Iraola continues to impress at Bournemouth despite losing key players such as Antoine Semenyo

Crucially, Bournemouth are in a position where they will be able to invest in the summer without selling after being forced to cash in on assets like Dean Huijsen, Milos Kerkez and Antoine Semenyo over the past two transfer windows.

‌



As one of the clubs set to take stock of their managerial situation in the coming weeks, Manchester United have seen a significant upturn in results and performances since handing the reins to Michael Carrick.

It remains to be seen, though, if Carrick will land the job full-time, with United continuing to be linked with other coaches.

Football.title: Arsenal facing issue as Mikel Arteta targets free transfer for in-demand star
Football.content:
Arsenal facing issue as Mikel Arteta targets free transfer for in-demand star

Arsenal have started drawing up plans for the summer transfer window, as Mikel Arteta aims to add to even more depth to a squad that's currently fighting on all fronts

View 2 Images Julian Brandt will leave Borussia Dortmund this summer (Image: Vitalii Kliuiev/Getty Images)

Arsenal have joined Barcelona in the race to sign Julian Brandt on a free transfer. It was confirmed on Saturday that Brandt, 29, will leave Borussia Dortmund upon the expiry of his contract in June.

‌



His availability has alerted several clubs, with Aston Villa among those to be linked to the attacking midfielder. Now it's emerged that Arsenal have entered the frame.

‌



Sky Germany claim that Brandt wants to move abroad for the first time in his career and Arsenal are interested. But Mikel Arteta would need to convince the player to snub Barcelona, who are also reportedly keen.

‌



The Dortmund star is said to be fluent in Spanish and has a Catalan girlfriend, making the idea of a switch to Barca - where he'd work under ex-Germany manager Hansi Flick - particularly appealing. Arsenal could try matching Brandt's €8million (£6.9m) salary as well as offering him a significant signing-on bonus.

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Declan Rice hits back at critics of Arsenal's set piece tactics with cheeky comment READ MORE :

As things stand, Arsenal and Barcelona are yet to approach to the German's agent and father, Jurgen. Brandt was the subject of bids from two unnamed English clubs in the January transfer window, both of which Dortmund turned down as they remained hopeful of convincing him to sign a new contract.

‌



The Bundesliga giants wanted the 29-year-old to lower his salary in a new deal. Brandt wasn't interested, though, as confirmed by Dortmund's CEO of sport, Lars Ricken, following Saturday's 2-1 win away to Koln.

"There were open discussions, and both sides agreed that the expiring contract would not be extended," Ricken explained post-match. "He's played a few hundred games for Borussia Dortmund. I think we can only be grateful to him.

Should Arsenal sign Julian Brandt? Have your say in the comments section.

‌



View 2 Images Barcelona are reportedly Julian Brandt's first choice (Image: Alexandre Simoes/Borussia Dortmund via Getty Images)

"He played for us for seven years. He was also viewed critically at times. He provided another assist today. I believe he'll be 30 in a few weeks - we can reassess things. So, this could be an opportunity for both sides.

"We have ultimately reached an agreement and I believe we parted with a great deal of respect. But it’s also another opportunity for both of us."

Article continues below

Brandt joined Dortmund from Bayer Leverkusen in 2019 and helped the club win the DFB-Pokal in his second season. He was also part of Dortmund's squad that reached the 2024 Champions League final.

Football.title: Chelsea's stance on selling Liam Delap after scoring once since summer transfer
Football.content:
Chelsea's stance on selling Liam Delap after scoring once since summer transfer

Liam Delap has found it difficult in his debut year at Chelsea but the club internally are still confident he has all the makings of a top Premier League striker

View 2 Images Liam Delap moved to Chelsea in the summer - but has found it difficult to produce thus far (Image: Julian Finney)

Chelsea are still confident that Liam Delap has the quality to be a top striker in the Premier League - despite him scoring just once in the league.

‌



The Englishman made the move from Ipswich after their relegation with the Blues paying his £30million release clause. Delap opted to move to Stamford Bridge despite having several other clubs - Manchester United among them - keen on his services.

‌



Delap has found it difficult to rediscover the form of last term and, so far, has found goals hard to come by. He's also behind Joao Pedro in the pecking order, leading to questions over his Chelsea future after just a matter of months.

‌



But Sky Sports claims that senior figures at the club believe Delap’s struggles are down to injury setbacks and are more than willing to give him more time to prove his worth.

The England U21 international was injured in the win over Fulham back in August, sidelining him for months, and was then dealt another setback against Bournemouth in December. The plethora of options in Chelsea's frontline means that, even when fit, minutes will be a challenge.

What transfer guru Marina Granovskaia has done since Chelsea exit including surprise return READ MORE :

Chelsea star hands Liam Rosenior huge injury boost with training return before PSG clash READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



New Blues boss Liam Rosenior has seen Pedro hit top form, scoring a recent hat-trick in a win at Aston Villa. And whilst he acknowledges his red hot form he maintains that Delap is still someone who will get a shot - and is confident he can produce when it comes.

"You need a group," he said. "Joao [Pedro] is on fire, but that’s nothing on Delap. I expect Liam, when he does get his opportunity to take it, to show his quality. So it’s not comparing players. Liam will have his opportunities to play in big games. It’s up to him to take it, and I’m confident that he will."

View 2 Images Injuries have continued to halt the striker's progress (Image: 2025 Catherine Ivill - AMA)

‌



Jamie Carragher has suggested that Delap made the leap from Ipswich to Chelsea too soon, claiming that a team in between the two would've been the better move for his career.

He claimed: "I just think young players, when they’re on a journey and getting better… the jump from Ipswich and Chelsea was too big, he needed somewhere in the middle.

"Somewhere like Everton. If you play centre forward for Everton and play every game and know even if you have a few bad games you’re still going to play. Whereas at Chelsea he’s never sure he’s going to play, even if he starts. Sometimes the jump is too big, you need that team in the middle."

Article continues below

Football.title: Lossiemouth crowned new Queen of Cheltenham with Champion Hurdle triumph
Football.content:
Lossiemouth swept aside all rivals to claim a brilliant victory in the Unibet Champion Hurdle at the Cheltenham Festival.

The 7/5 favourite, who was targeted at this race over the Mares' Hurdle - a contest she has won twice - was one of three Willie Mullins-trained runners and the choice of stable jockey Paul Townend.

The grey mare's Cheltenham record was flawless going into Tuesday's feature race and remained so afterwards, never looking in any sort of trouble ahead of a six-and-a-half-length defeat of Brighterdaysahead as The New Lion came home in third.

Alexei ran a big race in fourth, just ahead of gallant defending champion Golden Ace.

"She has that mare's pedigree which gives her that bit of speed and the cheekpieces, I think, just made a huge difference," said Mullins.

"It made her concentrate that bit more and as horses get older they probably, like the rest of us, we start looking for ways out of doing hard work and this really invigorated her.

Image: Lossiemouth leaves Brighterdaysahead in her wake in the Champion Hurdle

"When I saw her working in them the other morning and when she pulled up, Paul pulled up and I spoke to him and we both had the same feeling that this was the way to go. And then the race looked so open we thought, why not let's go for it.

"We were all anxious until the third-last and then I saw her hitting the third-last spot on and I looked back at JP's (McManus) colours (The New Lion) and I thought, right, he's starting to come under pressure and he was the one I was worried about.

"To win the Champion Hurdle definitely outranks everything else she's done. But to come back here four years in a row is an achievement in itself but then to win four years is, she's gone Triumph, Mares' twice and then this, and it's been superb.

"Just 12 months ago State Man would have left her for dead. When State Man fell last year, he might have brought her down!"

Football.title: Warren: Usyk to fight Wardley-Dubois winner, then Fury | 'We're up for that!'
Football.content:
Promoter Frank Warren intends to open negotiations for Oleksandr Usyk to fight the winner of Fabio Wardley's world title clash with Daniel Dubois.

Usyk, the unified heavyweight world champion, with wins over Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua has proved himself the best in the division. Although he is now 39 years old, Usyk still plans on fighting three more times.

Next the Ukrainian will box Rico Verhoeven, a kickboxing superstar, on May 23. After that Usyk wants to fight the Wardley-Dubois winner and then have a trilogy bout with his great rival Tyson Fury.

Wardley and Dubois fight for the WBO heavyweight world championship on May 9 in Manchester and their promoter Frank Warren is open for the winner to box Usyk in a unification.

"We're up for that," Warren told Sky Sports. "There's a lot of frustration because Fabio wanted that [Usyk fight before]. But the fact that Oleksandr has come out and said he wants the winner of the two of them, it's very encouraging. And certainly for the two guys, it gives them a lot of incentive.

"We've got a great relationship with him and if we can do some business we'll certainly do it."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Fabio Wardley states he wants to fight Usyk and Tyson Fury after he has beaten Daniel Dubois

Warren also represents Fury and completing a Usyk trilogy remains a real possibility for the former champion.

"If he comes through the fight [against Wardley or Dubois], that he then wants to fight Tyson, I love that fight. The first two fights were close, very exciting fights, really exciting fights, I'd watch that all day long. And Tyson wants that fight. He's told me on numerous occasions he'd love that fight," Warren said.

For Fury fighting Usyk this year is potentially more likely than boxing Anthony Joshua.

"I don't know where AJ is, obviously we've spoken a lot about the terrible tragedy and the loss of his good friends, best friends and team members," Warren reflected.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player With Verhoeven set to fight Usyk for the WBC heavyweight title, relive when Verhoeven sparred Fury in the build-up to his triumph over Wladimir Klitschko back in 2015

"People have got to remember about the injuries that he's suffered. He's suffered physical injures but he's also suffered mental injuries in that fight. Maybe boxing, the fact of fighting will get him in a better place, I don't know.

"I don't see him fighting this year, or this year against Tyson. I may be wrong and I hope I am wrong but I don't believe he will."

The division, though, will remain active with Usyk's three-fight plan likely to come to fruition.

"This heavyweight division, as I keep saying, it's alight," Warren added. "They're great fights and we're front and centre in it. We are making the fights."

Watch Dubois-Harper and Scotney-Flores live on Sky Sports on Sunday April 5.

Football.title: England make one change for final Six Nations match against France
Football.content:
England have made just one change to their starting line-up to face France in their final Six Nations match on Saturday as Ollie Chessum comes in for Sam Underhill.

Underhill is set to make his 50th England appearance from the bench, as is Marcus Smith.

Underhill made his debut against Argentina in 2017, while Smith first represented England in 2021 against the United States.

Coach Steve Borthwick had made major alterations for his side's historic 23-18 loss to Italy, with nine personnel changes in light of back-to-back defeats to Scotland and Ireland.

You can change your settings at any time via the Privacy Options Unfortunately we have been unable to verify if you have consented to Twitter cookies. To view this content you can use the button below to allow Twitter cookies for this session only. Enable Cookies Allow Cookies Once

It marked the most changes ever made by England in the Six Nations era.

However, Borthwick is sticking to his guns this time around and the one switch sees Chessum in at blindside flanker. Tom Curry was a late withdrawal in that same position last week which saw Underhill move from the bench to starting and Chandler Cunningham-South come in as a replacement.

After starting with a win over Wales, England will be looking to avoid a fourth successive Six Nations defeat against title-hopefuls France who will be bidding to bounce back from their 50-40 loss to Scotland.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Sky Sports reporter James Cole analyses what has gone wrong for England as they head into the final round of the Six Nations

"It's a huge challenge under the lights in Paris against a very strong France side," said Borthwick.

"England vs France is one of the great rivalries in international rugby and we're looking forward to the occasion."

On Underhill and Smith's milestones, Borthwick commented: "Reaching 50 caps is a special achievement and a reflection of the consistency both Sam and Marcus have shown in an England shirt.

"They have both worked extremely hard for this milestone and it's a credit to them and the people who have supported them along the way."

Football.title: 'That will be optimistic' - Alonso still unsure if Aston Martin can finish races
Football.content:
Fernando Alonso says it would be optimistic to think Aston Martin can finish races despite a better-than-hoped-for Australian Grand Prix.

Aston Martin team principal Adrian Newey made a stunning revelation last Thursday when he said Alonso and Lance Stroll would be unable to complete more than half of the season-opener due to the risk of suffering permanent nerve damage from car vibrations.

Stroll did not even take part in qualifying but completed 43 of the 58 laps on Sunday, while Alonso retired after just 21 laps. A shortage of parts is the primary reason for Aston Martin's limited running, although vibrations also remain an issue.

"We need to take the positives and move forwards. Everyone is determined to change the situation and working really hard," Alonso told Sky Sports F1.

"I think probably from the situation we had in Bahrain, or Thursday in Australia, we are in a better position now. We tested a few things, discovered the window and chassis more than in Bahrain and we did the formation lap, the start and pit stops with both cars. All these things are normal for everyone, but we never did it in Bahrain. It was good data and we will be more prepared for China, for sure."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Adrian Newey breaks down how the vibration and battery issues are impacting Aston Martin

Asked if he felt he could finish this Sunday's Chinese Grand Prix, the Spaniard said: "That will be optimistic but we can try. Obviously, we are still short on parts and China is only this week.

"But for Bahrain, apparently there will be more batteries and stock so for China we can take more risk on Sunday."

Bernie on Aston Martin Sky Sports F1's Bernie Collins



“Alonso knows from where they are now, it's not going to be different in China. He knows it's not going to be an overnight fix.



“I was surprised at the openness of some of the stories that came out, some of the comments that came out in terms of how the drivers were suffering and how the vibrations were.



“I actually feel they did better in the race than I thought, because I expected they were going to retire pretty early. I actually had said ‘I don't think one of them is going to make the grid’ because they had missed qualifying.



“They'd missed many sessions up to that point, sort of both cars on the grid, and then they decided to pull both cars into the garage at various points, make some changes, send them back out. And that is probably the longest long run that they have ever done.”

Honda 'confident' Aston Martin can complete race distance

Honda had tremendous success with Red Bull, helping Max Verstappen to each of his four world titles, before returning as a fully-fledged engine manufacturer for this season with Aston Martin.

But, Newey revealed only around 30 per cent of the original Honda team are currently working with Aston Martin which he only became aware of in November last year.

Aston Martin completed the least number of laps in pre-season testing and have the challenge of being the only Honda-supplied team, so will gain less data compared to other teams about their power unit.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player A red flag was issued during day two of the Bahrain Test after Alonso's Aston Martin stopped on the track

Honda's trackside F1 general manager Shintaro Orihara said: "From a Honda point of view, Australia was definitely a very important point to check the improvement of battery reliability.

"After Bahrain testing, Aston Martin and Honda worked together very hard to find a solution. And that was incredibly hard work. The guys worked long hours to find a solution.

"Then we bring some countermeasures here and checked countermeasure effect on day one. Then we found the good progress comparing to Bahrain and then we implement the countermeasure to day two and day three.

"Then on Sunday, we build up a lot of mileage. From a vibration viewpoint, we are confident to cover the full mileage in the race, so that is a big improvement from Aston Martin and Honda. That is a very key point for Aston Martin."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Lance Stroll and Alonso react to Newey's fears that Aston Martin's vibration issues could cause 'permanent nerve damage'

Football.title: Luke Littler: Is world No 1's UK Open win an ominous sign?
Football.content:
Sky Sports Darts' John Part believes that Luke Littler's UK Open victory could be an "ominous sign" for the rest of the PDC field.

Littler picked up an 11-7 win over James Wade in what was a scrappy final to become just the fourth player ever to retain in Minehead alongside Raymond van Barneveld, Phil Taylor, and Michael van Gerwen.

It also continued an incredible 100 per cent success rate in winning major semi-finals, and 'The Nuke' has now won a remarkable 12 majors.

On the way to beating Wade, Littler had battled past Damon Heta, Kevin Doets, Gary Anderson, Danny Noppert, and Josh Rock in a tough weekend.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Winning moment as world number one Luke Littler beats James Wade to retain the UK Open in Minehead. Footage from PDC

It means he has picked up another major win without admittedly reaching his usually lofty heights, a sign for Part that even Littler's B game can compete with the top more often than not and until someone puts in a performance against him which "damages his confidence," the run will continue.

"It is ominous in that sense that he is winning everything anyway. He is not playing his very best, not consistently," Part told the Love the Darts podcast.

"He is managing games well in terms of getting the job done. I really admire that he's throwing the darts when needed.

Captivate Captivate , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enable Captivate cookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the This content is provided by, which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enablecookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the Privacy Options Unfortunately we have been unable to verify if you have consented to Captivate cookies. To view this content you can use the button below to allow Captivate cookies for this session only. Enable Cookies Allow Cookies Once

"But he just looks sloppy far too often as well. So, I am thinking even when he played Josh and I don't even know how Josh got ahead in that match but that could have really been fatal.

"These players who he is doing it against, they are going to remember the next time and they're going to be eager for another chance to walk into him because they're not getting just completely slaughtered.

"That is what you want to do as a world No 1. You don't want there to be any issues.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Speaking on Love The Darts, John Part previews the Night 6 clash between Gian van Veen and Luke Humphries in the Premier League

"The ideal thing is that the only shots for an out they get are the 161 or something and that is the only opportunities you'll let them have. That is when you're really playing superb darts.

"Although he isn't doing that, he is still winning, so we have to wait and see which way it's going to go.

"The problem is if someone doesn't get into him and do some damage and really actually bother his confidence, his confidence is going to get even higher.

"He will be thinking that he is not even playing his best and he is winning all these things."

Wade using PL absence as motivating factor?

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Speaking on Love The Darts, John Part assesses whether James Wade can win a major in 2026 after he was the runner-up at the UK Open

For the second year in a row in the UK Open final, Littler came up against Wade, who is off to a brilliant start in 2026 once again.

Many felt 'The Machine' was unlucky to miss out on this year's Premier League but if he continues on this current trajectory, he will be an undeniable pick next time around.

However, for Part, it could be that exclusion which is helping spur Wade on to the current form we are seeing.

"He is definitely on course. I think he had a little bit of a case to get in the Premier League," Part added.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player James Wade defeats Nathan Aspinall 8-6 in Hildesheim to win the first Players Championship event of 2026

"But I look at it - if he had got in the Premier League, would he be doing all this now?

"It's sort of like the response, the indignity, the outrage, whatever he's feeling inside has been a motivational factor for him as an amateur psychologist here.

"I am thinking that this has all probably worked out to the best for him.

"Certainly, if the Premier League is his deepest desire to get back in there, then if he just keeps this up, I don't see how he can miss if he gets a major this year.

"The way he is playing, you figure he would probably get something along the lines because he's playing very well."

Watch Night Six of Premier League Darts, from Nottingham, live on Sky Sports Action from 7pm on Thursday. Stream darts and more sport with NOW, contract-free

Football.title: Old Park Star toughs it out in Supreme as Brits land 1-2-3-4
Football.content:
Ante-post favourite Old Park Star battled to get his head in front and claim the Skybet Supreme Novices’ Hurdle, the opening race of the 2026 Cheltenham Festival.

Nicky Henderson's unbeaten hurdler came into the race as the 15-8 market leader under Nico de Boinville, and remained handily placed as Sober Glory made the running, with market rival Mighty Park tracking them for Ireland.

The latter dropped away quite quickly, however, and as the hill approached it was Mydaddypaddy who loomed and attempted to throw down his own challenge.

But he was unable to outdo Old Park Star, who pressed on again to come out on top by a length and a half from Sober Glory, with Mydaddypaddy a nose away in third.

Henderson, winning the race for a sixth time, received a raucous round of applause from those gathered around the winner's enclosure, and said: "We've been here a long time, so we've got to know each other!

Image: Nico de Boinville celebrates after Old Park Star took the Supreme Novices' Hurdle

"This is a race that's been kind to us over the years. It's always a good race and the best novice hurdlers are all coming out to play.

"You have to have a good horse and we're very lucky they've come along - it's a long time since our first one in River Ceiriog (1986).

"Constitution Hill was obviously freaky when he won it and then your Altiors and Shishkins went on to jump fences and I would imagine that will be next year's game for this horse.

"On a day we've said goodbye to Constitution Hill (over jumps), it's nice to come straight in and a bring on a new one we hope could be a star.

Image: De Boinville is greeted by the Cheltenham crowd after Supreme victory on Old Park Star

"These young horses keep you going and I'm not going anywhere unless they want to pension us off!

"The first race on the first day is the best one you can get and England are one up (over Ireland)! I don't know if we'll be saying that on Friday night, but we'll keep trying."

He added: "It got tough up there. Turning into the straight he had to quicken off what looked a good, solid pace - he had to be brave to come up the hill like that.

"He's still a big baby in our opinion - I think in another year he'll be a much better horse.

"He's quick and tough and his jumping is sound. He's got a huge future in front of him."

Football.title: Barton charged after alleged assault
Football.content:
Joey Barton has been charged in connection with wounding with intent after a man was seriously injured after an assault near a golf club at the weekend; the 43-year-old is due to appear at Liverpool Magistrates Court on Tuesday March 10

Joey Barton: Former footballer charged after alleged assault near a golf club in Merseyside

Former footballer Joey Barton has been charged in connection with wounding with intent, after a man was left seriously injured from an assault near a golf club at the weekend.

The victim is being treated in hospital for serious injuries to his face and body. He is said to be in a stable condition.

Barton, 43, and Gary O'Grady, 50, have been charged in connection with the assault.

They are due to appear at Liverpool Magistrates Court on Tuesday afternoon.

A spokesperson for Merseyside Police said: "We can confirm that two men have been charged following an assault in Huyton on Sunday night, March 8.

"At around 9pm, emergency services were called to Fairway to reports of a man having been assaulted near to Huyton and Prescot Golf Club.

"The victim was taken to hospital for assessment of serious injuries to his face and body. He is in a serious but stable condition in hospital.

"Two men were arrested. Detectives have now charged the following men with Section 18 wounding with intent: Joseph Anthony Barton, 43, of Widnes. Gary O'Grady, 50, of Huyton.

"Both men remain in custody to appear at Wirral Magistrates Court this afternoon, Tuesday 10 March."

Meanwhile, Barton has been told to pay Eni Aluko more than £300,000 in damages and legal costs after he was sued for libel.

The High Court heard the case had been stayed, with Barton to pay "substantial" damages and Ms Aluko's legal costs.

Barton did not attend the hearing following his arrest on Monday, with Gervase de Wilde, for Ms Aluko, telling the court that he "is currently being held in custody".

Former England footballer and pundit Ms Aluko brought legal action against Barton over two posts he made in 2024 on social media site X.

Mr De Wilde said that in the two X posts Barton had wrongly claimed she had "cynically sought to exploit her status as an alleged victim of racism and bullying", and that she was a hypocrite.

The barrister said that between January and August 2024, Barton published 48 posts relating to Ms Aluko on his X account, adding that he had "carried out a deliberately targeted public campaign of vilification".

Mr de Wilde said one post included an image of Ms Aluko's head superimposed onto serial killer Rosemary West's body.

He continued: "The campaign amounted to an attack on multiple aspects of her life and personality."

"Mr Barton's campaign caused Ms Aluko enormous distress. When she sought to defend herself against Mr Barton's conduct, and that of his followers, he criticised and attacked her for doing so," he added.

Mr de Wilde said Ms Aluko was worried that however she tried to stop the harassment, Barton "would exploit and manipulate it to use against her".

The barrister continued: "Mr Barton has now accepted that his campaign against Ms Aluko amounted to harassment and that he should not have made the publications."

According to a court order seen by the Press Association, Barton has been told to pay £339,000 - with the first £100,000 plus interest due by March 24.

Mr Justice Lavender granted Barton seven days to ask to change the order. Ms Aluko attended the London court on Tuesday and told PA after the hearing: "I'm glad it's the end."

Football.title: Arshdeep sanctioned by ICC after throwing ball at Mitchell
Football.content:
India bowler Arshdeep Singh has been fined by the ICC after throwing a ball at New Zealand batsman Daryl Mitchell in Sunday's T20 World Cup final.

Arshdeep petulantly launched the ball at Mitchell, striking him on the pads in the 11th over of New Zealand's run chase, during which he had been hit for two sixes.

A furious Mitchell gesticulated and advanced towards Arshdeep, but the bowler turned and walked back to his mark instead of apologising.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights of the T20 World Cup final between India and New Zealand in Ahmedabad as the home side prevailed by a whopping 96 runs.

It was left to India captain Suryakumar Yadav to step in and calm the situation before the confrontation escalated.

Arshdeep was found guilty of a Code of Conduct breach by the International Cricket Council for "throwing a ball (or any other item of cricket equipment) at or near a player in an inappropriate and/or dangerous manner".

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player First three-time Men's T20 World Cup champions, first side to win back-to-back titles, and first to take the trophy at home! India are your T20 World Cup champions!

Arshdeep, who later apologised to Mitchell at the end of the over, was fined 15 per cent of his match fee after accepting the sanction, which meant a formal hearing was not necessary.

The ICC confirmed that one demerit point had been added to Arshdeep's disciplinary record.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Nasser Hussain and Michael Atherton review India's World Cup triumph and what went wrong for New Zealand.

India became the first men's side to win the T20 World Cup three times and the first to claim back‑to‑back titles with an emphatic 96‑run victory over New Zealand in Ahmedabad.

Football.title: Galatasaray v Liverpool: Champions League last-16, first leg – live
Football.content:
Key events 36m ago The teams

47m ago Preamble Show key events only Please turn on JavaScript to use this feature

45s ago 13.11 EDT Arne Slot gives his views on TNT Sports. “You can feel what it means to these [home] fans … the good thing is we’ve experienced it already, so it’s not new … I hope that’s going to help us … we are playing at Anfield as well, when our fans will show what support means … [in the league phase] we conceded after three minutes a big chance … not long after a soft penalty … that changed the game … they were able to use the time to their advantage … gave the fans and the players a lot of momentum and energy … that made it very very difficult.” Share

14m ago 12.57 EDT Pre-match postbag. “Hoping to see Slotto run out to the centre circle and plonk a dirty great YNWA flag there to show he, us and them all mean business” – Ian Copestake He’s making an oblique reference to this iconic moment in Galatasaray history, isn’t he, readers. The video below is well worth six minutes of your time. Not least because Graeme Souness’s pay-off at the very end is priceless. Share

28m ago 12.43 EDT Liverpool make five changes to their starting XI from the 3-1 FA Cup win at Wolves last Friday. Alisson is injured so Giorgi Mamardashvili takes his place in goal. Milos Kerkez replaces Andy Robertson at left back. Ibrahima Konaté returns in central defence, so Joe Gomez moves to right back, with Curtis Jones dropping to the bench. Rio Ngumoha and Cody Gakpo make way for Florian Wirtz and Hugo Ekitike. Share

36m ago 12.36 EDT The teams Galatasaray: Cakir, Singo, Sanchez, Bardakci, Jakobs, Torreira, Lemina, Baris Yilmaz, Gabriel Sara, Lang, Osimhen.

Subs: Sen, Guvenc, Sallai, Icardi, Sane, Yunus Akgun, Elmali, Gundogan, Kutucu, Asprilla, Ayhan, Boey. Liverpool: Mamardashvili, Gomez, van Dijk, Konate, Kerkez, Gravenberch, Mac Allister, Salah, Szoboszlai, Wirtz, Ekitike.

Subs: Woodman, Misciur, Jones, Gakpo, Robertson, Frimpong, Nyoni, Nallo, Morrison, Ngumoha. Referee: Jesus Gil Manzano (Spain). Share Updated at 13.07 EDT

Football.title: Two more Iranians seek asylum in Australia after football team flies out – reports
Football.content:
Two more members of the Iranian women’s football team have reportedly sought asylum in Australia after refusing to board a flight back to their home country after competing in the Women’s Asian Cup tournament.

A plane left Sydney airport on Tuesday night local time for Malaysia, reports said, with players and staff, ending a dramatic two days when five players were granted asylum after refusing to return home.

But that number rose to seven on Tuesday night, according to multiple reports, when two more members of the group decided to remain in Australia.

Reports said the squad left Sydney at 10.43pm local time on Malaysia Airlines flight MH140 bound for Kulala Lumpur but it was not clear what route they would take back to Iran after that because the country’s airspace is closed due to the Middle East conflict.

The team had arrived in Sydney just after 8pm on Tuesday evening local time after leaving the Gold Coast where they had played their final game of the tournament on Sunday – a 2-0 loss to the Philippines.

View image in fullscreen Supporters of the Iranian players wait for news at Sydney airport on Tuesday night. Photograph: Sarah Wilson/EPA

Following the granting of asylum to the first five, supporters had gathered at the airport in the hope that more the players or staff would try to remain in Australia. The speculation had built after the team became embroiled in controversy for refusing to sing the national anthem at their opening game 10 days ago, prompting threats of reprisals for “traitors” amid the US-Israeli attacks on the country.

But the supporters remained frustrated as the team exited the plane from the back and were escorted onto a bus surrounded by airport staff and Australian Federal Police.

When it became clear they were exiting via the back of the plane and onto a bus, the supporters shone their torches through the airport window. Supporters believed they saw someone from inside the bus appear to shine a torch light back. Some of the supporters were crying.

After the bus left, there was a tense conversation outside the gate between the supporters and AFP officers. The supporters told the police that the torch shining back meant the person wanted help.

Five players, led by captain Zahra Ghanbari, were formally granted protection in Australia by home affairs minister Tony Burke early on Tuesday morning. The group – who have been given temporary humanitarian visas, which offers a pathway to permanent residency – has already been given an offer to train with A-League Women club Brisbane Roar.

Farak, one of the supporters, came to the airport with a recording which she said was of one of the player’s mothers, telling her to stay in Australia. She said she received it via a friend in Iran and had planned to play it via a speaker when the player exited the plane.

View image in fullscreen A supporter of the women at Sydney Airport. Photograph: Sarah Wilson/EPA

“I’m pretty sure if she hears this she will want to stay,” Farak, who asked to use only her first name, told Guardian Australia before the players boarded the bus.

She was devastated the player did not get the chance to hear the recording.

“It’s so sad,” she said. “What if something happens to this girl?”

Protesters had earlier tried to block the departure of the players’ bus outside their Gold Coast hotel before it continued on and dropped the players off at the Gold Coast airport bound for Sydney.

Government offering protection

Advocates connected to the group believed more – including possibly some staff – were considering staying in Australia.

Burke had said on Tuesday morning the other team members have been provided with the option to stay. “We are making sure there are further opportunities where if people want to make a request [to stay] they get that opportunity,” Burke said.

View image in fullscreen Protesters demonstrate outside the Royal Pines resort as a bus carrying players from the Iranian women’s soccer team and staff departs. Photograph: Russell Freeman/Reuters

Amnesty International Australia’s Zaki Haidari said the players must be given an opportunity to speak to officials at the airport. “Customs and immigration officials, as well as Australian Border Force officers, have an opportunity to intervene and remind the team of their rights to seek protection in Australia,” he said, adding the offer of protection from the Australian government should be extended to players’ families in Iran.

Refugee advocate and former Socceroo Craig Foster said the players must be given independent legal advice before they leave the country. “We have a duty of care to these women now, from between now and when they may leave, that they’re all provided with that opportunity,” he said on the ABC.

Legal experts have expressed concern over possible offences committed under “exit trafficking” legislation, which prohibits the movement of people in and out of Australia using coercion.

View image in fullscreen The five Iran players granted asylum with Home Affairs minister Tony Burke. Photograph: Australian Department of Home Affairs/AFP/Getty Images

NSW Anti-Slavery Commissioner James Cockayne called for “accountability” for people “undertaking criminal violations of Australian law while in Australia”. He wrote to Australian Federal Police (AFP) commissioner Krissy Barrett on Monday night, referring “a case of suspected exit trafficking for immediate investigation”.

He said members of the Iranian team, CCTV and other eye-witness accounts could help determine whether crimes have been committed. “There’s certainly a duty to protect human rights under international law, and that includes robust investigation of credible allegations like we see here. Whether the investigation concludes that there is a criminal offence is for the investigating authorities to determine.”

He warned the decision to investigate might not only be that of the AFP. “There are obviously some complex geopolitical and diplomatic sensitivities that will be factors in government decision making on this issue.”

The AFP issued a statement on Tuesday afternoon, confirming they have been “providing assistance” to officials from the Department of Home Affairs for the past three days. “The AFP acknowledges the community leaders, not-for-profit groups and ordinary Australians for their assistance and advocacy in this matter. The AFP will not be making further comment at this stage.”

Prime minister Anthony Albanese has come under international pressure around his handling of the matter, amplified by the intervention of US President Donald Trump through social media posts in the early hours of Tuesday.

Albanese said it remained a “delicate” situation, but it will be up to the players to reach out for help. “It is up to them. But we say to them, if you want our help, help is here, and we will provide that,” he said.

Organisers have been the target of criticism for allowing the situation to get to this stage. The president of Fifpro Asia, Beau Busch, said a human rights assessment should have been undertaken prior to the tournament.

Daniel Ghezelbash, director of the Kaldor Centre for International Refugee Law at UNSW said people’s lives depend on these kinds of protocols. “It is well-documented that major sporting events enliven significant human rights risks, and a situation like the Iranian women’s team was a foreseeable eventuality.”

Burke said on Tuesday morning the group given protection broke out in a spontaneous chant of “Aussie, Aussie, Aussie, oi, oi, oi” to celebrate receiving their Australian papers.

Football.title: ‘So much disrespect’: outrage grows over postponement of Women’s Africa Cup of Nations
Football.content:
On 13 February, Patrice Motsepe, the president of the Confederation of African Football (Caf), promised that this year’s Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (Wafcon), scheduled to be played in Morocco between 17 March and 4 April, would go ahead as planned. One of the reasons he had to make that statement was the 2024 tournament had been postponed for a remarkable 19 months, until July 2025.

That supposedly solemn presidential promise was broken on 5 March, 12 days before the start of the tournament, with many of the teams – including Nigeria, the defending champions, Cameroon and Ghana – playing friendlies across Africa and Asia to prepare for the showpiece, which also determines which teams get to represent the continent at next year’s World Cup.

“After discussions between Caf and its partners, Fifa and other stakeholders, Caf decided to reschedule the dates of the Wafcon 2026, to 25 July–16 August 2026; to ensure the success of this important women’s competition, in the light of certain unforeseen circumstances,” Caf said in its statement. The Guardian asked Luxolo September, the Caf head of communications, to clarify what those unforeseen circumstances were, but received no reply.

The recurring uncertainty about Wafcon, which has been continually disrupted since 2020, has enraged African women’s football. “It’s not really fair,” Desiree Ellis, South Africa’s coach, told the South African Broadcasting Corporation as rumours were circulating that the tournament would once again be postponed. “I’m just going to raise a question with this Wafcon: would this be happening with the men’s game?”

Ellis’s frustration is shared by an executive committee member of the Nigeria Football Federation, who did not want to be named. “It is a huge shame that the women’s game is treated with so much levity on the continent,” they said. “How can Caf repeatedly treat the Wafcon so shabbily, with so much disrespect towards the players?

“Before the postponement, we played two friendly games against Cameroon in Yaoundé to prepare. This was to help our buildup to the tournament. All that is squandered now. We have to start all over again.” As Nigeria’s Rinsola Babajide, who plays for Roma, bluntly put it on a social media post: “It’s actually embarrassing at this point.”

View image in fullscreen Rinsola Babajide of AS Roma. Photograph: Insidefoto/LightRocket/Getty Images

It was a double blow for Ghana’s Black Queens, who were in the United Arab Emirates for the four-nation Pink Ladies Cup and got caught up in the Middle East war, an experience Kim Björkgren, the team’s Swedish coach, described as “frightening”.

“The whole situation [with the conflict] has been difficult to handle. The days have been moving in the wrong direction,” he told Moving the Goalposts from Dubai, before they left the country. “It affected our players in different kinds of ways. Some people are stressed, some people are more relaxed, some struggle to sleep and some people can. Sometimes, you hear the bombs during the night.

“We are very disappointed [about the postponement of the Wafcon]. We had been planning for it for a long time.”

It is not only players and coaching staff who have been affected. Many journalists had made plans to cover the tournament too. Firdose Moonda, a Guardian contributor, was to travel from Kolkata in India to Morocco, with her return home to South Africa being the final leg of her journey. Moonda has now had to buy a new ticket to return home. “Flight prices are more than double at the moment … I’m so frustrated with this … Caf should carry this cost,” she says.

Gayton McKenzie, South Africa’s sports minister, agrees with Moonda. “Caf should strongly consider booking flights for all journalists that can prove that they lost money, [as the] media is going through a very rough period currently, financially. This will just be the right thing to do.”

McKenzie also suggested that the hosts have been playing politics with the staging of Wafcon and that the hosting crisis was deliberately caused to embarrass Motsepe because of Morocco’s loss to Senegal in the final of the men’s Afcon in January. He said that South Africa was prepared to host the tournament should Morocco abandon the responsibility.

The Moroccan football federation, the host association of Wafcon, declined to comment on why it was unable to honour its commitment to Caf and whether the rescheduled tournament in July would, in fact, take place in the north African country.

Mid-level Caf staff charged with Wafcon preparations have also had to abruptly stop what they were doing. One official in Cairo, who wished to remain anonymous, said: “We expect members of the executive committee to hold the top people in administration to account. Bestine Kazadi Ditabala [of DR Congo, Caf’s fourth vice-president] is the head of the women’s football committee. We would like to hear her voice on this postponement.”

With the rescheduled Wafcon to begin six days after the men’s World Cup final on 19 July, and during the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, taking place from 23 July to 2 August, it would be a tall order for it to receive the global attention it deserves.

It is a pathetic situation that Motsepe, as well as key Caf staff, such as Véron Mosengo-Omba, the general secretary, must take full responsibility for, as governance incompetence has brought about this miserable state of affairs.

Get in touch

If you have any questions or comments about any of our newsletters please email moving.goalposts@theguardian.com.

Football.title: João Pedro shining brightest as Chelsea brace for PSG reunion
Football.content:
João Pedro had been a Chelsea player for less than two weeks when he faced Paris Saint-Germain in the final of the Club World Cup last summer. The settling-in period was intense. The forward had an impactful substitute appearance when he made his debut in the quarter-final win over Palmeiras, struck a clinical double when Fluminense were downed in the semi-finals and then, on a thrilling, sweaty afternoon in New Jersey, delivered the coup de grace when Chelsea became world champions thanks to a stunning demolition of PSG.

It was 3-0 when João Pedro lifted a clever finish over Gianluigi Donnarumma in the 43rd minute, and the manner of the humiliation was hard for PSG to accept. Heads were scrambled as the newly crowned European champions felt their aura of invincibility ebb away at the end of an epic season. João Neves was shown a red card for a tangle with Marc Cucurella – who else? – and the loss of discipline even involved Luis Enrique, the PSG manager, appearing to slap João Pedro in the face when a mass brawl broke out at full-time.

No doubt there will be scores to settle in round two when the sides meet in the first leg of their last-16 Champions League tie at the Parc des Princes on Wednesday night. For PSG, it is a chance to show that losing to Chelsea was a one-off at the end of a draining, trophy-laden campaign. Motivation is unlikely to be an issue, even if Luis Enrique insists that revenge is not on the agenda. Do not expect a warm reaction from the locals if Chelsea do another of those pre-match halfway-line huddles. Chelsea, with nine red cards this season, will need cool heads if they are to head back to London with hope before next week’s second leg.

It is tough to call. PSG have underwhelmed this season – they are top of Ligue 1 by a point after losing 3-1 to Monaco last Friday, were plunged into a playoff after finishing 11th in the Champions League mega-table and have looked weary since the Club World Cup. Injuries have taken a toll and new signings have not worked out. There have been flashes of infighting and the jury remains out on the decision to sell Donnarumma to Manchester City and replace the Italy goalkeeper with Lucas Chevalier.

View image in fullscreen João Pedro has flourished since Liam Rosenior was appointed Chelsea’s head coach. Photograph: Jacob King/PA
